reasoning: The market predicted a 52% chance of the ceasefire breaking as of August 21, 2025. An intense 12-day war between Israel and Iran concluded with a US-brokered ceasefire on June 24, 2025. Despite both sides expressing doubts and low-level frictions appearing immediately after the ceasefire, searches for July and August reveal no confirmed drone, missile, or air strikes between the two countries' sovereign territories since the truce took hold. With 10 days of peace passing since the August 21 polymarket estimate, time decay slightly reduces the probability from 0.52 to around 0.49, assuming a roughly constant hazard rate over the remaining four months (122 days) of the year.
